title: Stelis retusa
taxonomy: Stelis retusa is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Stelis, which is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae within the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Stelis retusa is native to Central America, specifically in Costa Rica and Panama. This species is often found growing in tropical rainforests and cloud forests.

The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. intro: Stelis retusa, also known as Stelis, is a type of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Stelis has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which may aid in treating conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis retusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Stelis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its traditional uses and applications may vary depending on the local culture and region.
